sections.heading: 📸 Cameras & Vision System
items: Why are cameras not detected at all? Hot-plugging after launch is not supported — cameras must be plugged in BEFORE launching NEXUS. Go to Settings and try different CAM_L_IDX and CAM_R_IDX values (0, 1, 2, 3, 4) until both feeds appear.
Why is only one camera showing? Ensure CAM_L_IDX and CAM_R_IDX are not identical. If both show the same feed, one index is wrong.

Why do the cameras fail to open simultaneously? This is often a USB bandwidth issue. Avoid plugging both cameras into the same USB hub; use separate physical USB ports on the motherboard. You can also try lowering the RESOLUTION to [640, 480] in Settings.

Why is depth estimation always 0.0m? Depth estimation requires a valid stereo calibration file. If the Dashboard shows '⚠️ UNCALIBRATED RAW', you must run PY CALIBRATION first. Also, ensure the right camera is functioning, as disparity requires both feeds.

Why are depth measurements completely inaccurate? The target might be outside the reliable depth range of 0.5–5m. Alternatively, the baseline (distance between cameras) might be too small (< 4cm), causing low disparity and poor accuracy.

sections.heading: 🔌 Hardware & Serial Communication
items: Why is the ESP32 failing to connect? Check Device Manager → Ports (COM & LPT) for 'USB-SERIAL CH340' and ensure that exact port is in Settings. If it's missing, install the CH340 driver from WCH.
Port is correct, but still 'PermissionError'? Close Arduino IDE Serial Monitor, PuTTY, or any other terminal connected to the COM port. Only one application can own a COM port at a time.

Why are the servos weak, jittery, or unresponsive? Insufficient power supply is the most common cause. MG996R servos draw up to 2.5A at stall; do NOT power them from the ESP32 5V pin. Use an external 5V 3A regulator.

I added external power, but servos still jitter? The external power supply GND MUST be connected to the ESP32 GND to share a common reference. Also, check if AIM_SMOOTHNESS is set too high or if there is PWM interference from nearby cables.

Why isn't the laser firing? Check the Dashboard to ensure the LASER toggle is ON. Verify the ESP32 is flashed with nexus_guardian_v2.2.ino. Ensure the laser is physically wired to GPIO 14. If the dot is faint, try powering the laser module with 5V instead of 3.3V.

sections.heading: ⚡ AI Engine & Performance
items: Why is the FPS dropping below 10 or severely lagging? You are likely using a PyTorch (.pt) model which runs on CPU and is 5–10× slower. Build and switch to a TensorRT (.engine) model for your GPU.

How else can I improve FPS? Lower the RESOLUTION to [640, 480] in Settings. Switch to a lighter model like yolo11m-pose (~25 FPS) or yolo11s-pose (~40 FPS) instead of yolo11x-pose (~15 FPS). Close background GPU-intensive apps.

Why does model loading fail (AI Engine Not Found)? The file at MODEL_PATH does not exist. If using an .engine file, it must be built on your current machine's exact GPU; engines are not portable. Ensure TensorRT is installed and in your PATH.

Why does the system constantly lose target lock? Your IOU_THRESHOLD might be too high, or the target is moving faster than MAX_Y_TOLERANCE allows. Try lowering IOU_THRESHOLD or increasing MAX_Y_TOLERANCE.
Why do I get 'Repeated No detection' warnings? The CONFIDENCE threshold may be too high, or the target is partially occluded. Try lowering CONFIDENCE in Settings to 0.35.

sections.heading: 🎯 Calibration (Stereo & Laser)
items: Why is the Stereo RMS Error > 1.0 px? The most common cause is a warped or bent chessboard; use a perfectly flat, rigid backing like foam board. Other causes include blurry images, glare on glossy paper, or capturing while the board is moving.

How can I improve my Stereo Calibration? Capture at least 20+ pairs of images. Ensure massive variety: tilt the board 20–45° in X and Y axes, capture at distances from 0.5m to 2m, and cover all four corners of the frame.

Why does the laser hit the center accurately but miss at the edges? You did not collect enough points during Laser Alignment, or your coverage was clustered only in the center. Collect at least 12 points (20+ recommended) ensuring you include points near all four frame corners.

Do I need to redo full laser alignment for a 2-degree error? No. Use the PAN_TRIM and TILT_TRIM sliders in the Dashboard to apply a global offset (±18°) for small systematic misalignments. If the error is > 5° everywhere, you must redo full alignment.
When MUST I recalibrate? If the cameras were bumped or the pan/tilt assembly was physically moved relative to the cameras, the previous matrices are completely invalidated. You must recalibrate.
